_meta.design_doc: the 2026-07-07 squad-skeleton design review (internal to the donor; not shipped)
_meta.partition_detail: its clause-level partition companion (internal; not shipped)
_meta.what_this_is: THE canonical customization store (R8). Every {{HOLE}} in every template resolves from here. seat-manifest.tmpl.yaml DERIVES its per-seat fields from this file at P4 (one write direction, no dual mastery); the 3 script tables regenerate from the manifest; P7 diffs THIS file against a fresh scan/probe. Also the upgrade-mode (P7) baseline and the embedded-interview-metadata fossilization guard.

filled_by_legend.retro-loop: the Mentor/harvest loop over real deliveries (post-init)

_meta.map_valued_holes: SEAT_UUIDS, SEAT_MODELS, RUNTIME_UUIDS, SKILL_UUIDS, SETTINGS_PROFILE_PATHS, EFFORT_LEVER and MCP_SERVER_CATALOG are MAPS. Templates select one key per row with a dotted suffix, e.g. {{SEAT_UUIDS.Techlead}} or {{EFFORT_LEVER.antigravity}} (EFFORT_LEVER keys by runtime family) — the base name is the canonical hole; the suffix is a static key selector into its value map. Only the base names count against the 44.

baked_defaults_register.note: Overridable later (at P3 proposal or a soft-override knob), NEVER asked at init. These replace former interview questions (adversarial F4/F6/F7/F9/F10 + §10).
baked_defaults_register.BINDING_VETO_SEATS: STATIC, baked in card prose (F6): Reviewer-Security + Reviewer-Contract are BINDING vetoes; Reviewer-Architecture is a WEIGHTED coherence flag (not a hard veto); Validator is ADVISORY (never vetoes). Override at the P3 roster proposal.
baked_defaults_register.STRONGEST_FAMILY_TO_BINDING_VETO: STATIC default (§5): the strongest reviewer family is assigned to the binding-veto seat. Override at P3.
baked_defaults_register.MONITOR_THRESHOLDS: STATIC constants matching the donor card (F7): stall-idle 30 min, re-poke cooldown 40 min, single-flight lock staleness 15 min. Soft-override knobs later; do not gate init.
baked_defaults_register.WORKSPACE_LANGUAGE: STATIC English (F9) + one inline override comment on the Helper Tone clause. Not an interview question.
baked_defaults_register.ROUTE_GLOBS_UNIVERSAL: STATIC (F10): near-universal security globs + the docs-inert allowlist ship verbatim in route.sh; only module-specific additive globs are scanned into {{ROUTE_GLOBS}}.
baked_defaults_register.ARCH_THRESHOLDS: STATIC constants matching the donor rev-arch card: summon Architecture at >8 files OR crosses >=3 modules OR touches core orchestration/async/locking/queue/state.
baked_defaults_register.BYTE_CAPS: STATIC policy (F3): measured at P4 (assembled bytes + ~5% headroom), keyed by seat, unknown seat FAILS CLOSED; the init linter hard-fails by default. Never the donor's hand-tuned numbers.
baked_defaults_register.RESEARCH_MCP_ON_LEAD: STATIC default (§10): research MCP servers stay on the Lead seat.
baked_defaults_register.DEDICATED_SQUAD_PER_PROJECT: STATIC default (§10): one dedicated squad per project.
baked_defaults_register.CONDITIONAL_SKILLS_BY_SCAN: STATIC default (F5): browser-e2e / llm-gateway-security / mcp-tool-authoring are auto-included by scan surface, not an owner byte-tradeoff question; runbook notes prune-later.

_meta.fossilization_guard: Every probe hole is stamped re_validate_per_engine_version:true and every scan hole is re-derivable at P1. P7 re-runs scan+probe, diffs fresh values against the `value` fields here, and asks the owner to confirm drifted globs/CI-names/ports/engine-facts before re-emitting affected artifacts. This file's stored values ARE the P7 upgrade baseline — the anti-Init-Fossilization loop (F11). Earned content is NOT a hole and is not restorable from here; P7 preserves it via the fenced-EARNED extract-and-reinject merge (R1).
_meta.no_smuggled_specificity: donor_example fields below are the ONLY place donor literals are permitted anywhere under manifest/ (per the packaging rule). They are the installer's reference for what a filled value looks like — never copied into an emitted artifact.

SEAT_UUIDS.name: SEAT_UUIDS
SEAT_UUIDS.donor_example: map role->agent-uuid: {Techlead: <uuid>, Builder: <uuid>, QA: <uuid>, Reviewer-Security: <uuid>, Reviewer-Contract: <uuid>, Reviewer-Architecture: <uuid>, Validator: <uuid>, Mentor: <uuid>, Coach: <uuid>, Monitor: <uuid>, 'Multica Helper': <uuid>}

PROJECT_BOARD_UUID.name: PROJECT_BOARD_UUID
PROJECT_BOARD_UUID.donor_example: <uuid> (the board every `multica issue create` targets; alias PROJECT_ID/BOARD_PROJECT_UUID)

CONTROL_ISSUE_KEY.name: CONTROL_ISSUE_KEY
CONTROL_ISSUE_KEY.donor_example: ACM-24 (single issue homing validation_ledger + pending_harvest + review/cost streams + Monitor's single-flight lock; aliases CONTROL_ISSUE_ID/VALIDATION_LEDGER_ISSUE/HARVEST_LEDGER_ISSUE/VALIDATOR_LEDGER_TICKET). The AP_DESC autopilot tick-prompt templates on this (R4).

WATCHDOG_AUTOPILOT_UUID.name: WATCHDOG_AUTOPILOT_UUID
WATCHDOG_AUTOPILOT_UUID.donor_example: <uuid> (the */20 stall autopilot; Lead arms at readiness PASS, Monitor self-pauses on idle; aliases WATCHDOG_AUTOPILOT_ID/MONITOR_AUTOPILOT_ID). STD+ only — no watchdog at MIN.

SKILL_UUIDS.name: SKILL_UUIDS
SKILL_UUIDS.donor_example: map generic-skill-name->skill-uuid (ONLY the generic skills ship; the donor's harvested project-skills and api-wire-oracles are EARNED, never seeded): {vertical-slice-issues: <uuid>, self-improve: <uuid>, skill-harvest: <uuid>, review-quality-metrics: <uuid>, llm-gateway-security: <uuid>, edge-case-hunter: <uuid>, migration-schema-safety: <uuid>, architecture-review-method: <uuid>, repomix-context-scoping: <uuid>, browser-e2e: <uuid>, eval-harness: <uuid>, root-cause-first: <uuid>, builder-dev-loop: <uuid>, mcp-tool-authoring: <uuid>, safe-refactor: <uuid>, reviewer-panel-degradation-recovery: <uuid>, squad-measurement: <NEW — authored at build time (R11), no donor id>}

SETTINGS_PROFILE_PATHS.name: SETTINGS_PROFILE_PATHS
SETTINGS_PROFILE_PATHS.donor_example: map profile-name->install-path for the two hardened seats (the donor's home paths NEVER ship — R3; the installer writes fresh paths on the target machine): {validator-hardened: ~/.config/multica-validator-hardened.json, claude-restricted: ~/.config/multica-claude-restricted.json}

OWNER_MENTION.name: OWNER_MENTION
OWNER_MENTION.donor_example: [@Quang Tran](mention://member/<owner-member-uuid>) — the member mention link the harvest/handoff seats notify; a member mention notifies the human and spawns no run (aliases PRODUCT_OWNER_MEMBER/OWNER_MENTION_URI)

SEAT_MODELS.name: SEAT_MODELS
SEAT_MODELS.donor_example: resolved BY CAPABILITY CLASS (§5) — never hardcoded; P0 ranks the account catalog. map role->model: {Techlead: claude-opus-4-8, Builder: claude-sonnet-5, QA: openai/gpt-5.5, Reviewer-Security: openai/gpt-5.5, Reviewer-Contract: openai/qwen3.7-max, Reviewer-Architecture: 'Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)', Validator: claude-sonnet-5, Mentor: claude-opus-4-8, Coach: 'Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)', Monitor: openai/glm-5.2, 'Multica Helper': '' (inherits runtime default)}

EFFORT_LEVER.name: EFFORT_LEVER
EFFORT_LEVER.donor_example: per-runtime effort lever (the flag shape differs by runtime; the LEVEL, e.g. 'high', lives per-seat in the manifest): {claude: '--thinking-level <level>', codex: '--thinking-level <level>', opencode: '--custom-args ["-c","model_reasoning_effort=<level>"]' (cliproxy; provider-prefixed model id), antigravity: 'the (<Level>) token IS embedded in the model name — no separate flag; do not lowercase it'}

MCP_SERVER_CATALOG.name: MCP_SERVER_CATALOG
MCP_SERVER_CATALOG.donor_example: SHIP BROAD-THEN-PRUNE (never the donor's already-pruned outcome — that's EARNED by the measurement loop). Broad catalog: agentmemory, context7, deepwiki, exa, fff, gitnexus, perplexity, playwright. The donor later pruned morph-mcp (0 calls) and composio (broke a run) — do NOT pre-prune; the STANDARD-tier usage sweep prunes (F12).

MEMORY_BACKEND_URL_API.name: MEMORY_BACKEND_URL_API
MEMORY_BACKEND_URL_API.donor_example: the memory-server endpoint + its tool-call names (backend-specific — R12): {backend: agentmemory, url: http://localhost:3111, tool_calls: [memory_lesson_recall, memory_smart_search, memory_lesson_save]}. The donor endpoint never ships (R3); the constitution MEMORY clause consumes the tool-call names, not literals.

RUNTIME_SKILL_AUTOLOAD.name: RUNTIME_SKILL_AUTOLOAD
RUNTIME_SKILL_AUTOLOAD.donor_example: per-runtime bound-skill autoload behavior: {claude: 'does NOT auto-load bound skills — READ them from disk with an imperative Read line', antigravity: 'does NOT auto-load — READ from disk', opencode: 'natively invokes bound skills (delivers AND loads)'}

RUNTIME_MCP_MOUNT.name: RUNTIME_MCP_MOUNT
RUNTIME_MCP_MOUNT.donor_example: per-runtime MCP mount point: {antigravity: 'per-agent mcp_config is INERT — rides the ambient global registry ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json owned by no script; assert-critical-servers-present (non-fatal warn)', claude: 'per-agent mcp_config honored', opencode: 'per-agent mcp_config merges OVER global'}

ENGINE_WAKE_SEMANTICS.name: ENGINE_WAKE_SEMANTICS
ENGINE_WAKE_SEMANTICS.donor_example: engine wake facts (the #1 stall class — RE-VALIDATE per engine version): a completed task run is TERMINAL and the runtime does NOT redeliver the wakeup; an end-marker + a full mention link IS the wake; a plain @Name or edited comment fires nothing; native child->done wakes the parent.

ENGINE_CONCURRENCY_AND_TIMING.name: ENGINE_CONCURRENCY_AND_TIMING
ENGINE_CONCURRENCY_AND_TIMING.donor_example: shared-pool size + timing (per-seat fan-outs are DERIVED strictly BELOW the pool, never the raw value — see derived.VALIDATOR_FANOUT, R6): {shared_pool: 4 slots (Lead/Builder/Mentor/Validator share), queued_offline_threshold: '>5 min ⇒ runtime offline', max_attempts: 2 (infra auto-retry once), stall_timings: '~5 min / ~20 min', monitor_cadence: 'every ~20 min'}

RUNTIME_QUIRKS.name: RUNTIME_QUIRKS
RUNTIME_QUIRKS.donor_example: grab-bag of engine/runtime-version quirks to re-probe (aliases SMART_SEARCH_QUIRK/MEMORY_SEARCH_QUIRK/POST_VERDICT_HELPER_FACT/RENDERER_SUPPORTS_DETAILS_FOLD/RUNTIME_RUN_FUSE/RUNTIME_RETRY_SEMANTICS/FAILED_RUN_ERROR_TAXONOMY/TRIGGER_MISS_BUG_PRESENT/MODEL_CONTEXT_ADVANTAGE): {smart_search: 'ignores the project arg', opencode: 'empty-output-completed detection', antigravity: 'npx-hang; ignores --max-turns; NOT auto-retried — post BLOCKED before the flaky call exhausts', renderer: 'supports HTML <details>/<summary> fold', trigger_miss_bug: 'present? (the post-dispatch-mention-no-run class)'}

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.donor_example: acme (the product/repo name; alias REPO_NAME on reviewer/validator identity lines)

MEMORY_PROJECT_SLUG.name: MEMORY_PROJECT_SLUG
MEMORY_PROJECT_SLUG.donor_example: acme (the agentmemory project scope for recall/save; alias PROJECT_SLUG)

ISSUE_KEY_PREFIX.name: ISSUE_KEY_PREFIX
ISSUE_KEY_PREFIX.donor_example: QUA (the board's issue-key prefix, e.g. ACM-123; the NO-GITHUB-APP clause + post-verdict usage example reference it — R4)

MODULE_GLOBS.name: MODULE_GLOBS
MODULE_GLOBS.donor_example: the repo's module/package globs, e.g. extensions/acme-* (alias EXTENSION_NAMES in safe-refactor)

TEST_CMD.name: TEST_CMD
TEST_CMD.donor_example: bun test (ONE canonical hole — dedupes 5 variants across cards/skills; alias TEST_COMMAND)

BUILD_VERIFY_CMDS.name: BUILD_VERIFY_CMDS
BUILD_VERIFY_CMDS.donor_example: the build/verify command set, e.g. tsc + bun test + vite build (aliases BUILD_CMD/BUILD_UP_COMMAND/FRONTEND_BUILD_CMD/UI_BUILD_COMMAND)

VCS_CLI.name: VCS_CLI
VCS_CLI.donor_example: gh (the VCS-host CLI, from `git remote -v`; swap for another host's CLI; the PR-exists + auth gates use it)

REPO_ABS_PATH.name: REPO_ABS_PATH
REPO_ABS_PATH.donor_example: ~/Code/acme (the gitnexus `repo:` arg on every tooling line; a smuggled coupling — never STATIC; alias GITNEXUS_REPO_PATH)

BASE_BRANCH.name: BASE_BRANCH
BASE_BRANCH.donor_example: origin/staging (the feature-branch base; also route.sh's diff base — F1: an unresolvable base must raise a loud usage error, never crash exit-128; aliases FEATURE_BASE_BRANCH/STAGING-BASE)

FEATURE_BRANCH_PATTERN.name: FEATURE_BRANCH_PATTERN
FEATURE_BRANCH_PATTERN.donor_example: the feature-branch naming pattern, e.g. feat/<slug> (alias BRANCH_NAMING)

TARGET_BRANCH.name: TARGET_BRANCH
TARGET_BRANCH.donor_example: staging (the final-PR merge target; counted once in scan — interview Q5 only CONFIRMS the scanned value, R13; alias MERGE_TARGET_BRANCH)

CODE_GRAPH_TOOL.name: CODE_GRAPH_TOOL
CODE_GRAPH_TOOL.donor_example: gitnexus (the code-graph/impact tool; degrades to file-search when absent; aliases CALLER_IMPACT_TOOL/CODE_INTEL_MCP_TOOLS)

CI_BASELINE_REVIEWER.name: CI_BASELINE_REVIEWER
CI_BASELINE_REVIEWER.donor_example: rs-pr-reviewer (the external CI reviewer bot the squad's review is additive over; scan the actual check names, never guess — the G1 lesson; aliases EXTERNAL_PR_BOT/CI_BASELINE_REVIEWER_NAME)

ROUTE_GLOBS.name: ROUTE_GLOBS
ROUTE_GLOBS.donor_example: MODULE-SPECIFIC additive globs ONLY (near-universal security globs like **/auth/**, **/gateway/**, .github/** and the docs-inert allowlist ship STATIC — F10). Donor module globs example: **/outreach/**, extensions/acme-multiagent/**. (aliases SECURITY_ROUTE_GLOBS/CONTRACT_ROUTE_GLOBS/SECURITY_PATH_GLOBS/ARCH_PATH_GLOBS/SECURITY_TRIGGER_GLOBS/CONTRACT_TRIGGER_GLOBS/DOCS_INERT_ALLOWLIST/BREADTH_THRESHOLDS/BLAST_RADIUS_GLOBS)

E2E_HARNESS.name: E2E_HARNESS
E2E_HARNESS.donor_example: the whole E2E bundle (inert without a mockable boundary): hermetic-mock + real-LLM commands, mock-LLM port, gateway host:port, mock client API, fault-injection schema, retry cap, smoke/teardown cmds, test-only dir, package manager, app-boot cmd + port range. Donor: make e2e-*, mock-LLM :18800, gateway :18789. (aliases E2E_BRINGUP_CMD/E2E_HERMETIC_CMD/E2E_REAL_CMD/REAL_LLM_CMD/MOCK_LLM_CMD/MOCK_LLM_PORT/GATEWAY_HOST_PORT/MOCK_LLM_CLIENT_API/FAULT_INJECTION_SCHEMA/RETRY_CAP/SMOKE_COMMAND/TEARDOWN_COMMAND/TEST_ONLY_DIR/PACKAGE_MANAGER/APP_BOOT_CMD/APP_PORT_RANGE/REAL_LLM_MAKE_TARGETS/ADMIN_SEED_METHOD/MOCK_OUTBOUND_ENDPOINT/SINGLETON_PORT/PORT_OVERRIDE_ENV/ALT_PORT_EXAMPLE)

UI_SURFACE.name: UI_SURFACE
UI_SURFACE.donor_example: the web-UI surface (CONDITIONAL — also gates the playwright MCP grant + the browser-e2e skill; whole bundle inert when absent). Donor: the .tsx dashboard / AG-UI + its port + headless auth header + identity mechanism + RBAC test file. (aliases UI_EXTENSION_PATH/UI_PORT/UI_BUILD_COMMAND/HEADLESS_AUTH_HEADER/IDENTITY_MECHANISM/RBAC_TEST_FILE/BROWSER_SKILL_NAME)

SQUAD_NAME.name: SQUAD_NAME
SQUAD_NAME.donor_example: acme-dev (interview Q1 — the squad's display name)

SEAT_PREFIX.name: SEAT_PREFIX
SEAT_PREFIX.donor_example: AC- (interview Q1 — every seat name except the platform-native Helper is SEAT_PREFIX + a STATIC role suffix; e.g. AC-Techlead. All derived.<ROLE>_SEAT_NAME tokens resolve from this)

ROSTER_TIER.name: ROSTER_TIER
ROSTER_TIER.donor_example: FULL — interview Q2. The recorded value must be EXACTLY one of MIN / STD / FULL (the README display names MIN-VIABLE/STANDARD map to MIN/STD; tier-gating and drift-lib's _tier_rank compare the enum, so never record a display name or a suffix like '(+ Coach opt-in)' — Coach opt-in is the manifest's separate coach-opt-in flag). Gates which seat rows the manifest emits. The donor squad is the full 11-seat roster.

EXECUTION_BOUNDARY.name: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
EXECUTION_BOUNDARY.donor_example: interview Q3, HARD gate (vague answer blocks init) — the irreversible-action class the squad must NEVER touch. Donor: never touch the VPS / deploys / production infra; the pipeline stops at the final PR. (absorbs IRREVERSIBLE_ACTION_CLASS + DEPLOY_TARGET_NAME — R13)

MERGE_AUTHORITY.name: MERGE_AUTHORITY
MERGE_AUTHORITY.donor_example: interview Q4, HARD gate — the ONE human who is sole merge authority (also the product owner). Distinct from OWNER_MENTION (the probed mention:// link). Donor: the human workspace owner. (alias MERGE_AUTHORITY_NAME)

PROTECTED_BRANCHES.name: PROTECTED_BRANCHES
PROTECTED_BRANCHES.donor_example: interview Q5 (scan-seeded), HARD gate — the branches an agent must never push to directly. Donor: staging, main.

DEPLOY_ON_MERGE.name: DEPLOY_ON_MERGE
DEPLOY_ON_MERGE.donor_example: interview Q6 (confirm — the scannable half comes from the deploy workflow, F8): does the human's `merge` reply double as deploy approval? i.e. does merge->{TARGET_BRANCH} fire a deploy workflow. Answer: yes / no + which workflow.

EARNED.name: EARNED
EARNED.donor_example: NOT a fill target — the 1 slot family shipped EMPTY on purpose (R1). Fenced regions <!-- EARNED:slot --> … <!-- /EARNED --> the Mentor/harvest loop fills from real deliveries: the security bug-class catalog + '#1-2 bug family' (rev-security), the arch layer-chain + named races (rev-arch), the llm-gateway sink inventory, Coach's worked example + calibration teach examples, the wake-stall incident ref (constitution), the ACM-N/AC-* incident anecdotes (builder-dev-loop, reviewer-panel-degradation-recovery), PIPELINE §8 changelog + §9 see-also, and all {project}-* harvested skills. P7 preserves live EARNED content via extract-and-reinject; a re-emit that shrinks a non-empty region HARD-FAILS.

extra_probes.note: Required at P0 but deliberately NOT among the 44 canonical holes: they are never {{...}} template tokens — scripts read them straight from this file. See reference/engine-probe-checklist.md § Extra P0 probes.

PLATFORM_INSTRUCTION_LIMIT.name: PLATFORM_INSTRUCTION_LIMIT
PLATFORM_INSTRUCTION_LIMIT.donor_example: a single-seat instruction-body byte ceiling (the donor's backend timed out near a ~21 KB card) plus a batch-vs-individual apply note

derived.note: Computed at P4/P5 from the holes above — legal in templates, NOT counted in the 44. Documented so consumers and P7 diffs can resolve them.

HELPER_SEAT_NAME.rule: STATIC 'Multica Helper' — the ONE seat whose name is platform-native, NOT prefix-derived (bias rule 5)

ARCH_SEAT_MODEL.rule: map-select {{SEAT_MODELS.Reviewer-Architecture}} — the reviewer card's identity-line model token

CONTRACT_SEAT_MODEL.rule: map-select {{SEAT_MODELS.Reviewer-Contract}} — the reviewer card's identity-line model token

SECURITY_SEAT_MODEL.rule: map-select {{SEAT_MODELS.Reviewer-Security}} — the reviewer card's identity-line model token

MONITOR_SEAT_UUID.rule: map-select {{SEAT_UUIDS.Monitor}} — the Monitor's own-UUID self-ID token (used to exclude itself from stall candidates)

SEAT_MENTION_DIRECTORY.rule: one line per emitted seat: '[@' + <name> + '](mention://agent/' + {{SEAT_UUIDS.<Role>}} + ')' from the manifest; this is the constitution MENTION DIRECTORY listing (kills triple-maintenance)

VALIDATOR_FANOUT.rule: STRICTLY BELOW the ENGINE_CONCURRENCY_AND_TIMING shared pool (R6) — reserve slots for Lead/Builder/Mentor first; NEVER the raw pool value. Donor: 2 derived below a 4-slot pool. Round cap 2, ceiling 6.

AP_DESC.rule: the Monitor watchdog autopilot tick-prompt, templated on {{CONTROL_ISSUE_KEY}} (R4): 'Run your liveness DETECTION sweep now, exactly per your Agent Identity instructions (all three stall shapes A/B/C, statuses todo/in_progress/in_review; detect-and-poke only; self-pause per step 7; lock on {{CONTROL_ISSUE_KEY}}).' — the donor embeds its own control-issue key here; audit-drift's lane-4 equality assert consumes this templated form, never a literal.

aliases.note: detail-file hole name -> resolution (canonical hole | derived token | STATIC default | EARNED fence). So P7 diffs against the older clause-level partition names resolve.
aliases.DOCS_INERT_ALLOWLIST: ROUTE_GLOBS (module part; universal allowlist ships STATIC)
aliases.WORKSPACE_LANGUAGE: STATIC default English (F9) — not a hole
aliases.BINDING_VETO_SEATS: STATIC default baked in prose (F6) — not a hole
aliases.ARCH_FILE_THRESHOLD: STATIC constant (>8 files) — not a hole
aliases.ARCH_MODULE_THRESHOLD: STATIC constant (>=3 modules) — not a hole
aliases.STALL_IDLE_THRESHOLD_MINUTES: STATIC constant (30 min, F7) — not a hole
aliases.REPOKE_GUARD_MINUTES: STATIC constant (40 min, F7) — not a hole
aliases.MIGRATION_HISTORY: EARNED fence (never ships — clean provisioner instead)
aliases.HARVESTED_SKILLS: EARNED fence ({project}-* skills — loop OUTPUT, never seeded)
aliases.LLM_APP_FP_GUARD: UI_SURFACE-sibling scan flag — ships conditionally when the scan detects an LLM/agent surface (folds into ROUTE_GLOBS/skill-conditional logic)
aliases.HARVEST_TRIGGER_TITLE: STATIC constant (the skill-harvest issue title convention) — not a hole
